title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Automation and Data Cli… · Behavior Case: Phase Budget Allocation
summary: A nightly reconciliation has a fixed run window and must divide deadlines across resolution, connect, headers, streamed body, parsing, and cleanup. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py phase budget allocation increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Propagate caller cancellation and deadlines through request creation, helpers, parsing, retries, and downstream work. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Predict Phase Budget Allocation · Theory Phase Budget Allocation
steps.instruction:
theory case — Python protocol evidence model has 900 ms end-to-end for DNS, connect, TLS, headers, streamed body, parse, and cleanup. A caller cancels during body read; allocate phase budgets, propagate the reason, stop owned work, and preserve unknown remote effect. Competency probe: Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
